task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view is centered on Brasilia, or the search bar/navigation tool shows 'Brasilia' being targeted.

task_completion.description: Zoom in to a scale of 500m or less.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scale bar or scale indicator on the map interface displays 500m or a smaller distance (e.g., 200m, 100m).
The 'field_observations' layer is highlighted or selected in the layer panel, or the editing mode indicates this layer is active.

task_completion.description: Create exactly four point features on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four distinct new point markers are visible on the map surrounding the center point of Brasilia.
Feature attribute forms, pop-ups, or map labels explicitly showing the names 'Corner 1', 'Corner 2', 'Corner 3', and 'Corner 4' being entered or displayed for the new points.

task_completion.description: The four points form a square surrounding the center point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spatial arrangement of the four created points roughly forms a square shape around the central Brasilia location.
Interaction with the save button, a checkmark confirming edits, or a notification indicating the project/layer changes have been saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the QField graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard QField app interactions (panning, zooming, tapping edit buttons) rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The points were created using the app's native digitizing tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of QField's crosshairs, digitizing menus, or native attribute entry forms.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the app by directly editing the SQLite/GeoPackage database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No SQLite command line interfaces, Python scripts, or third-party database viewer apps are visible in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required point names are 'Corner 1', 'Corner 2', 'Corner 3', and 'Corner 4' in the 'field_observations' layer. Note: The actual visual map marker for Brasilia in the provided database is located at lat -15.7801, lon -47.9297. The agent should visually center its points around this map marker. Accept visual centering around the Brasilia map marker as correct, even if backend scripts might strictly check against a different hardcoded coordinate.
